description_en: Slowly drop chin toward chest, then look upward. Move gently within a pain-free range.
Turn head to look over one shoulder, then the other. Keep shoulders still.
Tilt ear toward shoulder on each side without lifting the shoulder.
Glide the head straight back, making a double chin. Keep eyes level.
Tilt head toward one side, gently assist with the hand, feel stretch on opposite neck/shoulder.
Rotate head toward armpit and look down, gently assist with hand to feel back-of-neck stretch.
Tilt head to one side and gently rotate away, feel stretch along front of neck.
Anchor collarbone, side-bend head away, look slightly up to stretch side of neck.
Place forearms on a doorframe and step through until a stretch is felt across the chest.
Lying on back, gently nod chin and hold, activating the deep front-of-neck muscles.
Press head into hand in each direction without movement. Hold each contraction.
Squeeze shoulder blades together and down. Hold briefly, then release.
Lying face down, raise arms into Y, T, then W shapes to work the scapular muscles.
Extend arm to the side, alternately extending the wrist and side-bending the neck. Gentle, no pain.
Form an O-K sign near the eye, glide the nerve by moving the elbow and wrist. Keep it pain-free.
Lower arm with palm back, gently flex wrist and side-bend neck to glide the nerve.
Repeated chin retractions, gliding the head straight back to centralize symptoms.
Retract the chin, then extend the neck to look up. Return to start each rep.
Retract the head and use a finger on the chin to add gentle overpressure at end range.
Place a foam roller across the mid-back and gently extend backward over it.
On hands and knees, alternate arching and rounding the whole spine slowly.
Side-lying with knees bent, open the top arm across the body to rotate the upper back.
On hands and knees, reach one arm under the body and rotate, then reach up and open.
Reach overhead and side-bend away to feel a stretch along the side of the trunk.
Arm on a wall or doorframe, rotate body away to stretch the chest.
Corner stretch with the arm slightly higher to target the deeper chest muscle.
Lying face down, lift the chest off the floor using the back muscles. Keep neck neutral.
Pull a band or weight toward the trunk, squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Set and hold the shoulder blades down and back during arm movements.
Lying on back, press a weight toward the ceiling, reaching the shoulder blade forward.
Lying on back, gently flatten and arch the low back to rock the pelvis.
Pull one knee toward the chest, hold, then alternate. Keep the other leg relaxed.
Lying with knees bent, gently drop both knees side to side within comfort.
On hands and knees, alternate arching and rounding the lower back slowly.
Lie on back, lift one straight leg supported behind the thigh until a stretch is felt.
Half-kneeling, tuck the pelvis and shift forward to stretch the front of the hip.
Lying on back, cross ankle over opposite knee and pull the thigh toward the chest.
Seated or standing side-bend, reaching overhead to stretch the side of the low back.
Gently draw the lower belly inward without holding the breath or moving the spine.
Lying on back with knees bent, lift the hips into a straight line, then lower slowly.
On hands and knees, extend the opposite arm and leg, keeping the trunk steady.
On back with arms and knees up, lower opposite arm and leg while keeping the back flat.
Support on forearm and side of feet, lift the hips to hold a straight side line.
Seated, alternately straighten the knee and extend the ankle while tucking the chin.
Straighten the leg and dorsiflex/evert the ankle to glide the nerve gently.
Straighten the leg and point/invert the foot to glide the nerve, staying pain-free.
Prone or side-lying, bend the knee and extend the hip to glide the front-of-thigh nerve.
Lie face down and relax fully, allowing the low back to settle into extension.
Prop up on the forearms, letting the low back sag while the hips stay down.
Lying face down, press the upper body up with the arms while the hips stay on the floor.
Hands on the low back, gently arch backward while standing. Return to neutral each rep.
Lying on back, pull both knees toward the chest and hold to open the low back.
Flatten the low back into the floor by tilting the pelvis, then relax.
Sitting, bend forward reaching toward the floor to flex and open the low back.
Lean over and let the arm hang, gently swinging it in small circles and lines.
Hold a stick with both hands and use the good arm to guide the stiff arm overhead.
Hold a stick and guide the affected arm out to the side and back.
Seated at a table, slide the hand forward on a towel to ease the arm into elevation.
Walk the fingers up a wall to gradually raise the arm higher each time.
Pull the arm across the body with the other hand to stretch the back of the shoulder.
Side-lying on the affected side, gently rotate the forearm down toward the bed.
Bring the arm across the chest and pull gently with the opposite hand.
Elbow at the side, rotate the forearm outward against a band. Keep the elbow tucked.
Elbow at the side, rotate the forearm inward against a band toward the belly.
Raise the arms to shoulder height in the scapular plane (thumbs up), then lower.
Pull a band toward the body, squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Press a weight upward, reaching the shoulder blade forward at the top.
Do a push-up against a wall, then push a little further to round the upper back.
Extend the arm to the side and glide the nerve through wrist and neck motion.
Glide the ulnar nerve by combining elbow and wrist movement near the face.
Bend and straighten the elbow fully through a comfortable range.
With the elbow at the side, turn the palm up and then down slowly.
Straighten the elbow, bend the wrist down with the other hand to stretch the outer forearm.
Straighten the elbow, bend the wrist back to stretch the inner forearm.
Lower a light weight slowly into wrist flexion, then assist back up with the other hand.
Lower a light weight slowly into wrist extension, then assist back up.
Squeeze a soft ball or putty and hold, then release slowly.
Glide the ulnar nerve by bending and straightening the elbow with wrist movement.
Lower the arm, flex the wrist and glide the radial nerve through shoulder/neck motion.
Extend the arm and wrist, side-bend the neck away to glide the median nerve.
Bend the wrist up and down through a full comfortable range.
Move the wrist side to side, toward the thumb then toward the little finger.
Move the fingers through fist, hook, and straight positions to glide the tendons.
Open and close the fingers fully, spreading and bringing them together.
Straighten the elbow and gently pull the hand back to stretch the inner forearm.
Straighten the elbow and bend the wrist down to stretch the outer forearm.
Squeeze, pinch, and spread therapy putty to build hand and finger strength.
Squeeze a soft ball and hold, then release slowly.
Pinch putty or a pad between thumb and fingers, hold, then release.
Glide the median nerve through wrist and finger extension movements.
Glide the ulnar nerve using the O-K sign and elbow/wrist motion.
Lying or standing, bring the knee up toward the chest and lower it back down.
Slide or lift the leg out to the side and return, keeping the toes pointing up.
Seated or lying, rotate the lower leg outward to turn the hip inward.
Rotate the lower leg inward to turn the hip outward through a comfortable range.
Half-kneeling, tuck the pelvis and ease forward to stretch the front of the hip.
Cross the ankle over the opposite knee and draw the thigh toward the chest.
Sit with soles together (butterfly) or do a side lunge to stretch the inner thigh.
Lie on back and raise one straight leg, supporting behind the thigh until a stretch is felt.
Side-lying with knees bent, lift the top knee while keeping the feet together.
Side-lying, raise the top straight leg upward, then lower it slowly with control.
Lying on back with knees bent, lift the hips and squeeze the glutes, then lower.
Standing or prone, lift the straight leg backward using the buttock, then lower.
With a band around the legs, step sideways keeping tension and a slight squat.
Bridge while holding one leg straight, lifting the hips with the supporting leg.
Seated, glide the sciatic nerve by extending the knee with chin and ankle movement.
Side-lying, bend the knee and extend the hip to glide the front-of-thigh nerve.
Lying on back, slide the heel toward the buttock to bend the knee, then straighten.
Sit with the heel propped and let the knee straighten fully under its own weight.
Cycle at light resistance to ease the knee through a smooth, repeated range of motion.
Standing, pull the heel toward the buttock, keeping the knees together.
Lie on back and raise one straight leg, supporting behind the thigh until a stretch is felt.
Stand facing a wall, step one foot back and press the heel down with the knee straight.
Cross one leg behind the other and lean away to stretch the outer thigh.
Sit with the leg straight, tighten the thigh to press the knee down, hold, then relax.
Lying down, keep one knee locked straight and lift the whole leg, then lower slowly.
With a band behind the knee, straighten the knee fully against the resistance.
Slide down a wall into a partial squat and hold, keeping the knees over the toes.
Step up onto a low platform leading with the affected leg, then step down with control.
Rise from a chair without using the hands, then sit back down slowly.
Stand on one leg and hold steady; progress by closing the eyes or using a soft surface.
Balance on one leg and reach the other foot out in different directions, then return.
Perform shallow squats focusing on keeping the kneecap tracking over the toes.
Trace the letters of the alphabet in the air with the foot to move the ankle in all directions.
Lunge the knee forward over the toes with the heel down to bend the ankle.
Point and flex the foot fully to move the ankle up and down.
Stand facing a wall, step one foot back, keep the knee straight and the heel down.
Same as the calf stretch but with the back knee bent to target the deeper calf.
Pull the toes back with the hand, or roll the arch over a ball, to stretch the sole.
Loop a band around the foot and pull the toes up toward the shin against resistance.
Turn the sole inward against a band, keeping the leg still.
Turn the sole outward against a band to strengthen the outer ankle.
Rise up onto the balls of the feet, then lower the heels slowly.
Keep the heels down and lift the toes and front of the foot upward.
Balance on one leg and hold; progress by closing the eyes or using a cushion.
Stand on a wobble board and keep it level, controlling the ankle through small movements.
Balance on one leg and reach the other foot out in several directions, then return.
Straighten the leg and move the ankle to glide the tibial nerve gently.
Straighten the leg and point/invert the foot to glide the peroneal nerve.
